info.title: IRI Facility API reference implementation
info.description: A simple implementation of the IRI facility API using python and the fastApi library. For more information, see: [https://iri.science/](https://iri.science/)

post.description: Submit a job on a compute resource - **resource**: the name of the compute resource to use - **job_request**: a PSIJ job spec as defined here This command will attempt to submit a job and return its id.

put.description: Update a previously submitted job for a resource. Note that only some attributes of a scheduled job can be updated. Check the facility documentation for details. - **resource**: the name of the compute resource to use - **job_request**: a PSIJ job spec as defined here

units.description: Allocation units supported by this capability

self_uri.title: Self Uri
self_uri.description: The canonical URL of this object

Capability.title: Capability
Capability.description:
An aspect of a resource that can have an allocation.
For example, Perlmutter nodes with GPUs
For some resources at a facility, this will be 1 to 1 with the resource.
It is a way to further subdivide a resource into allocatable sub-resources.
The word "capability" is also known to users as something they need for a job to run. (eg. gpu)

CompressionType.title: CompressionType
CompressionType.description: Defines the type of compression to be used for compressing or extracting files.

volume_mounts.title: Volume Mounts
volume_mounts.description: List of volume mounts for the container

Container.title: Container
Container.description:
Represents a container specification for job execution.

Implementation notes: The value of gpu_cores_per_process in ResourceSpec should be used to determine
if the container should be run with GPU support. Likewise, the value of launcher in JobSpec should be used
to determine if the container should be run with MPI support. The container should by default. be run with
host networking.

JobAttributes.description: Additional attributes and scheduling parameters for a job.

executable.title: Executable
executable.description: Path to the executable to run. If container is specified, this will be used as the entrypoint to the container.

arguments.description: Command-line arguments to pass to the executable or container

inherit_environment.title: Inherit Environment
inherit_environment.description: Whether to inherit the environment variables from the submission environment

environment.description: Environment variables to set for the job. If container is specified, these will be set inside the container.

stdin_path.description: Path to file to use as standard input

pre_launch.description: Script or commands to run before launching the job

post_launch.title: Post Launch
post_launch.description: Script or commands to run after the job completes

JobState.title: JobState
JobState.description:
from: https://exaworks.org/psij-python/docs/v/0.9.11/_modules/psij/job_state.html#JobState

An enumeration holding the possible job states.

The possible states are: `NEW`, `QUEUED`, `ACTIVE`, `COMPLETED`, `FAILED`, and `CANCELED`.

ProjectAllocation.title: ProjectAllocation
ProjectAllocation.description:
A project's allocation for a capability. (aka. repo)
This allocation is a piece of the total allocation for the capability. (eg. 5% of the total node hours of Perlmutter GPU nodes)
A project would at least have a storage and job repos, maybe more than 1 of each.
